business.headline: Inside Fairfax's proposed $5.5 billion IDBI Bank acquisition and what's at stakeIf the proposed transaction goes through, it could be worth around $5.5 billion-$5.7 billion, making it one of the largest foreign investments ever in an Indian bank and one of the government's most significant privatisation deals in recent years.

India-UK social security pact: Will your PF balance grow under the new deal?While the India-UK trade pact has attracted attention for opening up markets and reducing tariffs, the social security agreement could have an equally meaningful impact for Indian professionals.

Top 10 changes as India-UK FTA kicks in: Cheaper cars, whisky, zero-duty exportsIndia's trade pact with the UK has come into effect, lowering duties and widening market access. The deal is expected to aid exporters, professionals and businesses while reshaping bilateral trade.

IBM's $70 billion wipeout: What triggered its worst stock crash in 58 years?IBM just suffered its worst stock crash in nearly six decades. What went wrong, and what does it reveal about the winners and losers of the AI race?
Dalal Street opens in green as banks lead gains, crude stays above $85The S&P BSE Sensex rose 530.01 points to 77,584.95, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 145.10 points to 24,197.15 as of 9:30 am.

Did AI help decide Meta's layoffs? Lawsuit makes a serious claimTwenty-six Meta employees have accused the company of using AI-assisted tools to score workers ahead of layoffs. The case could test how far employers can rely on such systems in workplace decisions.

From 500% to 100%: US eases tariff threat against India, China for Russian oil buysThe US Senate has updated a Russia sanctions bill by cutting the proposed tariff on buyers of Russian oil and gas. The change could ease pressure on major importers while giving Donald Trump room to waive penalties.

The changing face of India's gold crazeGold still holds a special place in Indian homes, but the reasons for buying it are quietly evolving. Is the yellow metal becoming less about tradition and more about investment?

IBM shares plunge 26% as AI spending shift hits software businessThe company has been trying to reduce its reliance on the cyclical mainframe business by focusing on its software unit including its high-margin Red Hat business, which helps firms run applications across different cloud providers.

India tightens import rules, bans goods produced using forced labourThe decision is expected to strengthen India's import regulations and encourage businesses to pay closer attention to their global supply chains.

Dalal Street ends lower as Brent crude tops $86 amid fresh Middle East tensionsThe S&P BSE Sensex declined 570.10 points, or 0.73%, to settle at 77,046.30, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 171.10 points, or 0.71%, to close at 24,039.90.

Byju's founder can't return to Singapore without risking jail after court rulingSingapore's High Court rejected Byju Raveendran's plea to suspend a six-month contempt sentence. The ruling keeps the term in force and adds to his cross-border legal battles.

Tata is still India's No.1 brand. But there's a new entrant in the top 10Tata Group retained its place as India's most valuable brand for the 18th straight year, while Adani Group entered the top 10 for the first time. The Brand Finance India 100 2026 report also showed stronger gains for companies investing in AI, digital infrastructure and renewable energy.
Crude oil rises to a 1-month-high above $86: Will petrol and diesel prices increase too?At the time of writing, Brent Crude was trading at $86.07 per barrel, up around 3.33% during the day.

Have you recieved this 'total TDS' message? Here's what it meansThe Income Tax Department has started sending 'Total TDS' SMS alerts to taxpayers across the country. Why are you receiving this message, and what does it mean for your ITR? Have a look.

Inflation jumps from 2.74% to 4.38% in 6 months: Why did it rise so sharply?Food inflation climbed to 5.32% from 4.78% a month earlier. Since food carries the highest weight in the inflation basket, higher food prices had the biggest impact on overall inflation.

Why South Korea's KOSPI, world's best-performing stock market, is suddenly crashingSouth Korea's KOSPI has slipped into a bear market after a record AI-driven surge led by Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix. The reversal has exposed heavy retail leverage, foreign outflows and regulator concerns over single-stock products.

EPFO simplifies PF transfers: What the new rule means for job switchersThe change has been introduced after EPFO shifted to its Centralised IT Enabled Services (CITES) platform, which is designed to bring member records and services onto a single centralised system.

SBI Funds Management IPO GMP hints at 16% listing gains, but should you subscribe?SBI Funds Management is India's largest asset management company (AMC) and manages the SBI Mutual Fund. It is a joint venture between State Bank of India and French asset manager Amundi.

Trump wants a 20% Hormuz toll. What it could mean for India's fuel billIndia is in a better position to weather a disruption than it was a few months ago, but any prolonged instability around Hormuz could still push up oil prices, freight costs and inflation.

entertainment.headline: Will break his fast with my own hands: Rakhi Sawant vows to meet Sonam Wangchuk Rakhi Sawant appealed to Sonam Wangchuk to end his fast and said she would go to Jantar Mantar to help him break it. Her message came as support from other public figures grew and concern mounted over his worsening health.

Nick Jonas knows every Bollywood gossip, break-up before Priyanka Chopra does On the Jonas Brothers' podcast, actor Priyanka Chopra said her singer-husband Nick Jonas often updates her on Bollywood gossip and celebrity break-ups. The chat turned playful as Nick admitted he ghost-follows unnamed gossip pages.

CM Vijay's Jana Nayagan delays son Jason Sanjay's debut directorial Sigma Reports say Jason Sanjay's Sigma may move from July 31 to August as Vijay's Jana Nayagan eyes a July 23 release. The shift has turned attention to Vijay's swansong and fresh talks around a perfect launch for his son's debut directorial.

When Zendaya got a rare 'perfect' from Nolan: What Tom Holland told India Today Actor Tom Holland reacted to his co-star, wife Zendaya receiving Christopher Nolan's rare 'perfect' on The Odyssey set during an exclusive chat with India Today in Mumbai. He said Nolan's appreciation for his performance was all the validation he needed.

Always wanted to be a mother, I'll give it my all: Samantha gets candid on pregnancy Samantha Ruth Prabhu said that she has long awaited becoming a mother as she prepares to welcome her first child with Raj Nidimoru. The actor said pregnancy has given her a new sense of strength and purpose.

Aamir Khan faces fatwa; UP cleric says marriage to Gauri Spratt is against Sharia A Uttar Pradesh cleric has objected to Aamir Khan's marriage to Gauri Spratt and issued a fatwa over their interfaith union. The remarks have stirred debate, while the actor and his team have remained silent.

Kriti Sanon cheers for Team India with rumoured boyfriend Kabir Bahia. Pics Actor Kriti Sanon enjoyed the India vs England ODI from the stands with her rumoured boyfriend, entrepreneur Kabir Bahia. Photos from the stadium quickly went viral on social media.

Why So Ji-sub's K-drama Agent Kim Reactivated is 2026's biggest surprise hit Agent Kim Reactivated has crossed 22 per cent ratings on SBS, with So Ji-sub driving its rise at home and on Netflix in India. The drama's success underscores the pull of emotionally rooted action and the trust viewers place in a long-established star.

Neither Gauri, Reena nor Kiran converted: Aamir Khan rejects 'love jihad' tag Aamir Khan has rejected 'love jihad' allegations and said his interfaith marriages involved no conversions. He married Gauri Spratt in an intimate ceremony at his Mumbai home on July 5.

Doesn't hold your hand: Matt Damon on Nolan's approach to films, Interstellar days Actor Matt Damon praised Christopher Nolan's unique filmmaking style in an exclusive interview. He recalled how the director preferred to keep shooting instead of celebrating a perfect take during Interstellar.

Beginner's guide to Christopher Nolan's films: How to decode his complex worlds The countdown to The Odyssey has begun, and so has the Christopher Nolan obsession. Here's why everyone loves his films, and where you should start.

Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey is courting controversy. Here are 4 reasons why From casting call to dialogues and from location choice to peak detailing, Christopher Nolan's new film, The Odyssey, has triggered its share of outrage.

Dhamaal 4 box office Day 4: Ajay Devgn's film ends first Tuesday on a high note Actor Ajay Devgn's Dhamaal 4 held its ground on Day 5 as Tuesday offers helped draw more audiences to theatres. The film is now closing in on a big box office milestone.

Nick Jonas reveals first message that sparked his romance with Priyanka Chopra Singer Nick Jonas opened up about the first X (then Twitter) message he sent to his wife, actor Priyanka Chopra. The couple revisited their awkward first DM exchange on the Hey Jonas podcast.

Why Matt Damon and Tom Holland can't get over India's 'rock concert' movie culture Actors Matt Damon and Tom Holland spoke about their admiration for Indian cinema and its audiences in an interview with India Today. Damon praised the joy of song and dance in Indian films, while Holland said cinema-going in India feels like a concert.

India needs you: Shabana Azmi urges Sonam Wangchuk to end his hunger strike Shabana Azmi has appealed to Sonam Wangchuk to end his indefinite hunger strike, saying India needs people like him to continue the fight against injustice. The actor urged him to stay healthy, adding that his guidance gives hope to students and that the country stands with him.

Would jump at the opportunity: Matt Damon, Tom Holland ready for Bollywood break Actors Matt Damon and Tom Holland said in Mumbai that they would gladly do a Bollywood film with the right story. The two even highlighted their admiration for Indian cinema's joy and audience energy.

Matt Damon, Tom Holland's India diaries: Bollywood dreams, Nolan and The Odyssey In an exclusive interview, actors Matt Damon and Tom Holland spoke about The Odyssey and working with Christopher Nolan while promoting the film in Mumbai. The conversation also turned to India, fan culture and Tom's interest in doing an Indian film.

Swara Bhasker meets Sonam Wangchuk on Day 17 of hunger strike, voices support Actor Swara Bhasker visited Jantar Mantar and met engineer-activist Sonam Wangchuk and CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke during the activist's indefinite fast. Her message of solidarity came as concerns over Wangchuk's health and appeals to end the strike intensified.

general news.headline: Sharad Pawar's NCP may back NDA's delimitation bill, breaking ranks with OppositionThe Sharad Pawar-led NCP is likely to back the NDA's Delimitation Bill if it guarantees a uniform 50% increase in Lok Sabha seats across all states, a move that could see the party break ranks with the Opposition.

I spent a night as a cockroach at CJP's Jantar Mantar protest. This is what I sawI stayed overnight at the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protest site at New Delhi's Jantar Mantar without revealing my identity. I spoke to the participants, ate the food being served and overheard the conversations around fasting activist Sonam Wangchuk. I also noticed the unease over absent CJP leaders.

IBM's $70 billion wipeout: What triggered its worst stock crash in 58 years?IBM just suffered its worst stock crash in nearly six decades. What went wrong, and what does it reveal about the winners and losers of the AI race?

Case filed against 4 influencers over videos targeting Nitin Gadkari for E20The FIR was filed on the basis of a complaint lodged by a BJP Social Media Cell functionary in Nagpur. According to the complaint, a YouTube video uploaded by Manish Kashyap contained misinformation meant to tarnish the minister's reputation.

When Zendaya got a rare 'perfect' from Nolan: What Tom Holland told India TodayActor Tom Holland reacted to his co-star, wife Zendaya receiving Christopher Nolan's rare 'perfect' on The Odyssey set during an exclusive chat with India Today in Mumbai. He said Nolan's appreciation for his performance was all the validation he needed.

Lost 8.5 kg, may die in 2 days: Plea seeks force-feeding of Sonam WangchukA petition has been filed before the Delhi High Court seeking medical aid for activist Sonam Wangchuk, whose hunger strike demanding the resignation of the Union Education Minister entered its 18th day on Wednesday.

PM Modi helped stop Putin from using nukes in Ukraine, claims Polish ministerPoland's deputy foreign minister said PM Modi helped dissuade Vladimir Putin from using tactical nuclear weapons in Ukraine in late 2022. He said Modi remains among the few leaders able to influence Putin.

From IIT to millionaire: Meet Siddhartha Saxena who earned Rs 77 crore in a single dayFrom IIT Kanpur to building a fast-growing AI startup, Siddhartha Saxena's journey is defined by innovation and calculated risk. The Merlin co-founder says he earned nearly Rs 77 crore (USD 8 million) in a single day, becoming a millionaire at just 26.

England vs Argentina semifinal: Messi finally faces the one team that eluded himEngland vs Argentina semi-final: England and Argentina do not meet as ordinary opponents. Rooted in Maradona's infamous 1986 quarter-final, this rivalry carries weight few international fixtures can match. Now Messi faces the one team that has always eluded him.

CBSE 3-language policy: SC questions if English can be considered indigenous languageThe Supreme Court refused to stay CBSE's Class 6 three-language policy while hearing challenges to its rollout. The bench's remarks on English and indigenous languages sharpened the wider debate over multilingual education and implementation.

Ethanol fact: India is trying to do with a jolt what Brazil achieved in decadesIndia's sprint to achieve 20% ethanol-blended petrol five years ahead of schedule has left people scrambling. While ministers keep citing the example of Brazil, the country took several decades to replace pure petrol while transitioning to flex-fuel vehicles. Here's the lesson from Brazil's transition.

Shashi Tharoor writes open letter to Jantar Mantar protesters, Sonam WangchukShashi Tharoor urged Sonam Wangchuk to end his indefinite fast, saying the protest had already stirred the nation's conscience and that students' concerns should now be raised in Parliament. He also appealed to the Centre to open a dialogue with protesters, even as Wangchuk refused to call off his fast.

Neither Gauri, Reena nor Kiran converted: Aamir Khan rejects 'love jihad' tagAamir Khan has rejected 'love jihad' allegations and said his interfaith marriages involved no conversions. He married Gauri Spratt in an intimate ceremony at his Mumbai home on July 5.

If women need to be 'expert mothers', why no one asks men to become 'expert fathers'?Anandiben Patel's call for women to become expert mothers before pursuing top careers has revived questions about unequal expectations. The debate has turned to why parenting excellence is demanded of women but not of men.

Inside Congress' Bihar project: Rahul Gandhi's organisational reboot faces revolt?The Congress Bihar unit's digital membership drive, touted as a model that could eventually be replicated across the country, is drawing criticism from within the party, with several senior leaders alleging that the new system effectively allows organisational posts to be secured through money rather than merit.

Is every second person ill in Mumbai? Why monsoons bring a dangerous disease backSome doctors are noticing a wave of influenza and respiratory illnesses in Mumbai. Health experts warn that not every monsoon fever is just a viral. The next time you step through ankle-deep floodwater and feel unwell, remember, it could be leptospirosis. Here's why one should seek a doctor's advice.

Teenage swagger: At 19, Lamine Yamal walks the talk, escapes the trolls in styleLamine Yamal's pre-match swagger was vindicated as Spain beat France 2-0 in the World Cup semi-final. His sharp display and Spain's control left the pre-tournament favourites chasing shadows.

Capgemini daycare operator alleges extortion plot, claims videos were stagedThe management of the Capgemini daycare in Bengaluru claimed the viral child abuse videos were deliberately staged by terminated employees as part of an extortion conspiracy.

They fired on Ram devotees, now talking about faith: Yogi Adityanath attacks SPSpeaking at Panchayat Aaj Tak, Yogi Adityanath said the government ordered a thorough probe into the Ram temple donations theft once the allegations surfaced. He also hit out at the Opposition for raking up the matter and questioned their motives.

Maharashtra FDA suspends licences of 3 Mumbai restaurants over hygiene lapsesThe latest crackdown comes shortly after the FDA ordered the closure of the iconic K Rustom ice cream parlour near Churchgate railway station after a surprise inspection allegedly found rats, flies and expired flavouring agents at the premises.

Zendaya isn't just playing Athena in The Odyssey. She dressed like one tooZendaya plays goddess Athena in Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey, and guess what, she embraced the character at the New York premiere of the film as well.

Satellite captures massive monsoon system knocking Bengal, Odisha. See picsINSAT-3DR satellite images have captured a massive cloud system over the northwestern Bay of Bengal. The development could revive the southwest monsoon and widen rainfall across eastern, central and northern India.

Woman pins student down as husband strangles him with scarf over video blackmailLured to the couple's home, the 20-year-old was allegedly ambushed in a pre-planned attack. Police said the husband, who had pretended to leave for work, returned secretly, pinned the student down by sitting on his chest while his wife restrained his hands, before strangling him with a scarf and then a cable.

Cyclospora outbreak: Taco Bell investigated as lettuce emerges as possible sourceAccording to a report by The Washington Post, US health officials are investigating whether Taco Bell restaurants may be linked to a major cyclosporiasis outbreak. While no connection has been confirmed, lettuce and salad greens have emerged as possible sources of the infection.

sports.headline: Selfless template gone, India back to old-fashioned grind ahead of 2027 World Cup India's commanding win over England in the first ODI was more than just a morale-boosting result after the T20I debacle. It also offered the clearest indication yet that Shubman Gill's India are embracing a more patient, calculated ODI template with the 2027 World Cup in South Africa firmly in mind.

Freeze Kane: Argentina fans pin hopes on bizarre superstition before England clash Argentina fans are sticking to their cherished cabalas ahead of the World Cup semi-final against England, believing rituals such as freezing Harry Kane's name and repeating matchday routines can help the defending champions reach another final.

Teenage swagger: At 19, Lamine Yamal walks the talk, escapes the trolls in style Lamine Yamal's pre-match swagger was vindicated as Spain beat France 2-0 in the World Cup semi-final. His sharp display and Spain's control left the pre-tournament favourites chasing shadows.

IND vs ENG: Sundar credits coach Gambhir for unlocking his batting potential Washington Sundar credited India head coach Gautam Gambhir and Gujarat Titans coach Ashish Nehra for helping him evolve as a batter after his unbeaten half-century guided India to a six-wicket victory over England in the first ODI at Edgbaston on Tuesday.

Atlanta Police tighten security for England vs Argentina World Cup semi-final The Atlanta Police Department (APD), in a statement on Tuesday, said it had enhanced security measures for Wednesday's FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final between England and Argentina at Atlanta Stadium, deploying additional personnel and resources to ensure the match passes off safely.

Stop Messi at all costs? England coach Tuchel considers masterplan for semi-final England coach Thomas Tuchel said he has considered using an old-school man-marking plan to contain Lionel Messi when his side face Argentina in Wednesday's FIFA World Cup semi-final.

FIFA chief Infantino under IOC scrutiny over Balogun World Cup ban reversal The International Olympic Committee (IOC) has received a complaint seeking an investigation into FIFA president Gianni Infantino's alleged role in reversing Folarin Balogun's World Cup suspension. It also accuses Infantino of repeatedly breaching political neutrality rules through his support for US President Donald Trump.

Argentina vs England: Coach Scaloni outlines formula to reach World Cup final Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni has urged his players to combine their trademark grit with their attacking quality when they take on England in the FIFA World Cup semi-final on Wednesday, insisting that determination alone will not be enough to secure a place in the final.

England vs Argentina semifinal: Messi finally faces the one team that eluded him England vs Argentina semi-final: England and Argentina do not meet as ordinary opponents. Rooted in Maradona's infamous 1986 quarter-final, this rivalry carries weight few international fixtures can match. Now Messi faces the one team that has always eluded him.

Lionel Messi's perfect-record referee to officiate Argentina vs England semi-final FIFA has appointed Ismail Elfath to officiate Argentina's World Cup semi-final against England in Atlanta. Messi has been unbeaten in all five matches involving the Moroccan-American referee.

How Pedri and Lamine Yamal manifested Spain's World Cup final into existence Pedri and Lamine Yamal's old Instagram post has resurfaced after Spain reached the World Cup final on 19 July. What once seemed cheeky now reflects the belief driving Spain's young side into a record-chasing title match.

Spain have found their 2010 World Cup spirit: De La Fuente after France winSpain beat France 2-0 in Dallas to reach the 2026 World Cup final, after which Luis de la Fuente said his side had rediscovered the spirit of 2010. The coach said the squad's unity, humility and collective mindset have put La Roja one win from a second world title.

Kylian Mbappe owns up to France's poor display vs Spain in World Cup semifinalKylian Mbappe admitted France were second best after a 2-0 World Cup semi-final defeat to Spain, saying Les Bleus fell short tactically and technically. France will now face the third-place playoff, Didier Deschamps' final match before his expected departure.

Mbappe, Dembele, Olise didn't turn up for France: Patrick Vieira on Spain defeatPatrick Vieira tore into France after their 2-0 World Cup semi-final defeat to Spain. He said the team's biggest players failed collectively as Spain's discipline exposed them.

Didier Deschamps questions referee, but France had bigger problems against SpainFrance vs Spain, FIFA World Cup semifinal: France coach Didier Deschamps questioned the referee after the World Cup semi-final loss to Spain but admitted Les Bleus were second best as Rodri and Spain's midfield dismantled their attacking threat.

Spain strip Mbappe-led France of their aura to reach World Cup finalFIFA World Cup 2026: Kylian Mbappe, Ousmane Dembele and Michael Olise had lit up the World Cup. Spain switched off the lights, producing their most complete display of the tournament to march into only the second World Cup final in their history.

FIFA fever goes traditional: World Cup themed dhotis become hot favouritesKerala's football fever has found a unique local expression during the FIFA World Cup, with football-themed dhotis flying off the shelves alongside team jerseys. Shopkeepers say Argentina, Brazil and Portugal-inspired mundus have become some of the most sought-after items this tournament.

Bumrah's sorcery, Axar's resilience hand India first win on UK tourIndia won their first match of the tour of United Kingdom on Tuesday, 14 July. Jasprit Bumrah, returning to the ODI team for the first time since November 2023, once again set the tone with his opening spell of the match.

Lord's century is a dream come true: Yastika Bhatia relishing historic 100Yastika Bhatia called her historic unbeaten 113 at Lord's a "dream come true" after becoming the first woman to score a Test century at the iconic venue. The landmark knock capped an inspiring comeback from the ACL injury that sidelined her in late 2025.

FRA 0-2 ESP: Spain outplay France to make it to World Cup finalFrance (FRA) vs Spain (ESP) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final: (FRA 0-2 ESP) Spain make another double substitution as Marcos Llorente replaces Pedro Porro and Nico Williams comes on for Alex Baena. With an 82nd-minute 2-0 lead, La Roja are freshening up their side as they close in on the World Cup final.

Tom Cruise to IShowSpeed headline FIFA World Cup 2026 closing ceremonyFIFA has confirmed Tom Cruise, IShowSpeed and a string of music stars for the World Cup 2026 closing ceremony in New York New Jersey. The pre-match show is set to cap the first 48-team tournament before the final decides the champions.

Another injury scare for India? Gurnoor Brar taken off in 1st ODIIndia suffered another injury scare during the first ODI against England after fast bowler Gurnoor Brar limped off the field while attempting to bowl his final over. The right-arm pacer had earlier sparked England's collapse with two quick wickets.

Watch: MS Dhoni melts hearts with sweet gesture for kid in stands at EdgbastonMS Dhoni stole the spotlight during the first ODI between India and England at Edgbaston, sharing a heartwarming moment with a young fan who offered him popcorn from the stands.

Promises unfulfilled, Ryan ten Doeschate wants to quit India coaching jobRyan ten Doeschate is keen to leave the Indian cricket team after the England tour. According to sources, he is unhappy that he never received the specialist fielding coach's role he was promised.

Shoulder injury continues to plague Sat-Chi as duo retire from Japan OpenSatwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty retired after the opening game of their Japan Open opener as Rankireddy's shoulder injury flared up again. The setback will make them skip the China tournament, while PV Sindhu gave India brighter news by moving into the second round.

Kuldeep Yadav pays the price for Gambhir's all-rounder obsession, againIndia left out Kuldeep Yadav for the first ODI against England and packed the XI with three all-rounders on Tuesday. Abhishek Nayar said the call sacrificed a wicket-taking middle-overs option for batting depth.

Erling Haaland returns from the US with a raccoon, breaks the internetDespite Norway's heartbreaking World Cup exit, Erling Haaland has stolen the global spotlight by returning home with a bizarre, whiskey-clutching taxidermied raccoon. Now, the internet is completely obsessed with his viral new Texas travel companion.

1st ODI Highlights: Washington, Axar guiding India towards victoryEngland (ENG) vs India (IND), 1st ODI Highlights: India are inching towards a win in the first ODI of the series. Washington Sundar and Axar Patel keep the innings steady after two quick dismissals of KL Rahul and Shreyas Iyer.

Friction between Gambhir and Agarkar? India urged to clear confusion after UK disasterDinesh Karthik said Gautam Gambhir and Ajit Agarkar appear out of sync after India's T20 defeats in Ireland and England. India lost T20I series in England and Ireland without even winning a game.

PV Sindhu dominates Wong Ling Ching to seal Japan Open Round 2 berthPV Sindhu made a dominant start to her Japan Open Super 750 campaign, defeating Malaysia's Wong Ling Ching 21-14, 21-11 to advance to the women's singles second round on Tuesday.

When Maradona fooled the world: Hand of God returns before Argentina vs EnglandFIFA World Cup 2026: As England and Argentina prepare to renew one of football's fiercest rivalries, the shadow of Diego Maradona's Hand of God still looms large. Technology may have transformed the game, but some World Cup memories simply refuse to fade.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
